core_model.structure_summary: Constrained minimization of an energy functional whose quadratic gradient part is augmented by nonlocal coefficient fields determined self-consistently as convolution-type functionals of the squared state, minus a potential term with exponential-type growth, over the sphere of fixed squared integral norm in two dimensions, the multiplier appearing in the stationarity system.
core_model.canonical_form: E(u) = \frac{1}{2}\int_{\mathbb{R}^2} \big[|\nabla u|^2 + (v_1^2 + v_2^2)\,u^2\big]\,dx - \int_{\mathbb{R}^2} G(u)\,dx \ \text{on } \Big\{\int u^2 = c\Big\}; \qquad -\Delta u + \mu u + v_0 u + (v_1^2+v_2^2)u = g(u), \quad \partial_1 v_2 - \partial_2 v_1 = -\tfrac{1}{2}u^2, \ \partial_1 v_1 + \partial_2 v_2 = 0, \ \partial_1 v_0 = v_2 u^2, \ \partial_2 v_0 = -v_1 u^2

variables.meaning: scalar function on the two-dimensional continuum whose constrained critical profile is sought
auxiliary fields determined nonlocally by first-order equations sourced by the square of the primary function
unknown multiplier enforcing the fixed squared-norm constraint
fixed value of the squared integral norm
energy functional whose constrained minimizer over a natural manifold is the sought ground state

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A stationary semilinear elliptic equation in the plane in which the state is multiplied by nonlocal convolution-type functionals of its own square (a cubic-order nonlocal self-interaction) and balanced against a linear multiplier term and a rapidly growing local nonlinearity, under a fixed-norm side condition.

notes: The elliptic system with self-consistent auxiliary fields is the standing-profile (single-frequency) reduction of a first-order-in-time gauge-coupled dispersive evolution system quoted as background; the paper works with the stationary constrained problem. The auxiliary fields can be eliminated via explicit convolution kernels, yielding a single nonlocal equation; an identity ties the v_0-weighted mass to twice the (v_1^2+v_2^2)-weighted mass. Supercritical exponential growth of the local nonlinearity is handled by a truncation family.
